name: Goal Setting Frameworks
description: SMART, HARD, GROW, and OKR frameworks for turning ambitions into actionable plans.
when_to_use: User states a vague goal that needs structure
Agent is decomposing a complex objective into tasks
Goal needs emotional commitment (HARD) or measurable tracking (OKR)

smart.name: SMART Goals
smart.description: The standard for turning vague ambitions into actionable plans
smart.best_for: Concrete, well-defined objectives that need clear execution plans

specific.question: What exactly will be accomplished? Who is involved? Where?
specific.test: Could someone else understand exactly what this means without clarification?

measurable.question: How will we know when it's achieved? What metrics define success?
measurable.test: Can progress be tracked with a number, percentage, or clear milestone?

achievable.question: Is this realistic given current resources, skills, and constraints?
achievable.test: Do we have or can we acquire what's needed? What's the biggest barrier?

relevant.question: Why does this matter? How does it connect to larger objectives?
relevant.test: If achieved, would it meaningfully move the needle on what matters?

time_bound.question: When must this be completed? What are the intermediate deadlines?
time_bound.test: Is there a specific date, not just 'soon' or 'eventually'?

smart.conversion_prompt: Take the user's goal and restate it passing all 5 SMART criteria. If any criterion can't be met, ask the user for that specific information.

hard.name: HARD Goals (Mark Murphy)
hard.description: Goals that drive deep emotional commitment through vivid connection
hard.best_for: When motivation is the bottleneck, not planning. For goals that require sustained effort through difficulty

heartfelt.question: Why does this goal matter deeply? What personal values does it serve?
heartfelt.prompt: Connect this goal to something the user genuinely cares about — not just outcomes, but meaning

animated.question: Can you vividly picture the end state? What does success look, feel, sound like?
animated.prompt: Paint a detailed picture of what life/work looks like when this goal is achieved

required.question: What happens if this doesn't get done? What's the cost of inaction?
required.prompt: Make the urgency concrete — not fear-based, but clarity about stakes

difficult.question: Is this goal stretching beyond the comfort zone? Does it require growth?
difficult.prompt: The goal should be challenging enough to require learning and adaptation, not so hard it's demoralizing

grow.name: GROW Model
grow.description: Structured coaching conversation for moving from current state to desired outcome
grow.best_for: One-on-one guidance sessions, self-reflection, when someone knows what they want but not how to get there

goal.purpose: Define what you want to achieve
questions: What would you like to accomplish?
What does success look like specifically?
How will you know when you've achieved it?
Is this goal within your control?
Where are you right now in relation to this goal?
What have you already tried?
What's working? What isn't?
What obstacles are you facing?
On a scale of 1-10, how far along are you?
What could you do as a first step?
What else? (ask this 3+ times)
What would you do if you had unlimited resources?
What would you advise someone else in this situation?
Which option excites you most?
Which option is most likely to succeed?
Which option will you pursue?
When specifically will you do this?
What might get in the way, and how will you handle it?
On a scale of 1-10, how committed are you?
What support do you need?
What's the very first step you'll take?

okr.name: OKRs (Objectives & Key Results)
okr.description: Ambitious objectives tracked with measurable key results
okr.best_for: Complex multi-faceted goals that need measurable tracking. Projects with multiple workstreams

rules: Difficult but achievable (70% hit rate is ideal)
key_results.scoring: 0.0 = no progress, 0.3 = some progress, 0.7 = good delivery, 1.0 = exceptional

okr.conversion_prompt: Help the user define 1 clear Objective and 3-5 measurable Key Results. Each KR should have a current baseline and target number.
